---
title: "Quantifying modern slavery risk in photovoltaic supply chains"
description: "A network-based assessment of forced-labour exposure across the global solar PV supply chain, from polysilicon refining to panel assembly."
date: 2024-09-14
categories: [solar-pv, supply-chain, forced-labour]
pub-author: "Rodríguez-Huerta, E. et al."
pub-journal: "Nature Sustainability"
pub-doi: "10.1038/s41893-XXX"
---

[← Back to Publications](../publications.html){.back-link}

<div class="label" style="margin-bottom: 16px;">Journal Article · Nature Sustainability · 2024</div>

## Abstract

The rapid scaling of solar photovoltaic (PV) deployment has surfaced a paradox: the same supply chains driving the energy transition also carry significant exposure to forced-labour risk, particularly in upstream polysilicon refining. We combine bilateral trade data, facility-level audits, and labour-rights indices to quantify modern-slavery risk across the global PV supply chain.

## Citation

> Rodríguez-Huerta, E., et al. (2024). Quantifying modern slavery risk in photovoltaic supply chains. *Nature Sustainability*, X(Y), pp. — pp. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41893-XXX

## Downloads

- [PDF (preprint)](#)
- [Supplementary materials](#)
- [Code & data (GitHub)](https://github.com/edgarrodriguezhuerta)

## BibTeX

```bibtex
@article{rodriguezhuerta2024slavery,
  title   = {Quantifying modern slavery risk in photovoltaic supply chains},
  author  = {Rodríguez-Huerta, Edgar and others},
  journal = {Nature Sustainability},
  year    = {2024},
  doi     = {10.1038/s41893-XXX}
}
```
